title: rhodiola rosea subsp. arctica
intro: Rhodiola rosea subsp. arctica, also known as Arctic rhodiola, is a subspecies of the Rhodiola rosea plant, which is a type of flowering plant native to the Arctic regions of Europe, Asia, and North America. Rhodiola rosea subsp. arctica has been found to possess medicinal properties, including adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory compounds, which are believed to contribute to its health benefits. The plant is known to help alleviate stress, improve mental clarity, and boost the immune system, as well as exhibit antioxidant and anti-fatigue properties. Rhodiola rosea subsp. arctica is typically harvested and prepared as a dietary supplement in the form of capsules, tablets, or tinctures, which can be used to support mental health and well-being.
